name: Eggplant-Filled Ravioli with Tomato Cream Sau
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon Basil, fresh, minced or 1 teaspoon dried, crumbled
1 cup Italian plum tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped or drained canned Italian plum tomatoes
recipeInstructions:
FOR FILLING: Peel eggplant and slice into 1/2-inch thick slices. Salt generously on both sides. Drain on paper towels for 45 minutes. Pat dry with paper towels. Cut eggplant into 1/2-inch cubes. Heat oil in a heavy large skillet over medium heat. Add onion and garlic and sauté until soft, about 5 minutes. Add eggplant and sauté until beginning to turn golden brown, about 15 minutes. Stir in wine, parsley and basil, scraping up browned bits. Cover and cook until eggplant is tender, about 3 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Transfer to a food processor or blender and puree until smooth. Cool completely.
FOR PASTA: Combine flour, eggs and salt in the food processor and mix well. With the machine running, add just enough water through the feed tube to form a ball. Remove from the processor. Cover the dough and let rest for 30 minutes. (Pasta dough can also be made by hand.) Cut dough into 4 pieces. Flatten one piece (keep the remainder covered), then fold in thirds. Turn pasta machine to the widest setting and run the dough through several times until smooth and velvety, folding before each run and dusting with flour if sticky. Adjust the machine to the next narrower setting. Run the dough through without folding. Repeat, narrowing rollers after each run, until the pasta is 1/16 to 1/8 inch thick, dusting with flour as necessary. Line baking sheets with towels. Set a sheet of dough on the work surface. Place about 1-1/2 teaspoons filling at 2-inch intervals along the sheet. Roll out the second piece of dough. Set atop the first sheet. Press down around the filling to force out air and seal the dough. Cut into 2-inch squares. Arrange on prepared sheets. Repeat with remaining dough and filling. Cover with a towel.
FOR SAUCE: Combine Madeira, wine and shallots in a heavy small saucepan. Boil until reduced to 1/4 cup, about 4 minutes. Add cream and lemon peel, grated, and boil 5 minutes, stirring occasionally. Stir in tomatoes. Season with salt and pepper. Cook ravioli in a large pot of boiling water until just tender but still firm to the bite, about 4 minutes for fresh. Remove with a slotted spoon; drain. Divide among plates. Spoon sauce over. Garnish with basil. Serve immediately. Serves 6.
DO AHEAD HINTS:  Ravioli can be prepared one day ahead and refrigerated or one month ahead, wrapped tightly and frozen. Do not thaw before cooking.  Sauce can be prepared one day ahead; refrigerate. EVEN EASIER:  Purchase sheets of fresh pasta for the ravioli or buy prepared ravioli to serve with the tomato cream sauce here.
